data.title: Lyric Health appoints Bill Schmitt as CGO to accelerate virtual care growth. New role to drive strategic growth efforts.
data.description: In McKinney, Texas, on January 30, 2026, Lyric Health, a prominent company specializing in AI-driven virtual care and holistic health guidance, revealed the selection of Bill Schmitt as their Chief Growth Officer (CGO). Tasked with spearheading the company's expansion initiatives, Schmitt steps into this novel position to drive Lyric's strategic growth efforts.

data.title: Britain's Royal Mail faces challenge as late letters raising concerns, amid criticism of its 500-year-old postal service.
data.description: Delays in mail delivery in the UK are causing frustration among residents and sparking criticism of the country's longstanding postal service, which has been in operation for over 500 years.

data.title: Chung-Ang University scientists in Seoul generate electricity using a Tesla turbine-inspired structure with practical compressed air.
data.description: A novel method of generating electricity without direct contact has been developed, utilizing compressed air and a Tesla turbine design in Seoul, South Korea. This innovation comes in response to the growing energy needs in multiple industries. Over the past few years, researchers have been investigating the technique, with remarkable findings. The approach aims to enhance energy production efficiency and minimize environmental impact. This development marks a significant step forward in the pursuit of sustainable energy solutions.

data.title: ParaZero receives second big order from defense corp for interception net-pods, boosting growth.
data.description: In Kfar Saba, Israel, on January 30, 2026, ParaZero Technologies Ltd. (Nasdaq: PRZO) disclosed a significant update. The aerospace defense company, known for its innovative autonomous solutions in the manned and unmanned aerial systems industry, shared news of securing another important contract. This time, they received a second major order from a leading global defense corporation for their cutting-edge interception net-pods.

data.title: Amplia Therapeutics releases Q4 2025 activities & cash flow updates.
data.description: In recent data reported, the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.5%, reflecting a decrease of 0.3% from the previous month. This shows a positive trend in job creation with over 200,000 new jobs added during the same period. However, the inflation rate has slightly increased by 1.8%, indicating a rise in overall price levels for consumer goods and services. Additionally, the stock market has experienced a notable growth with the main indexes climbing up by 2.5% on average. Overall, these economic indicators suggest a mixed outlook for the country's financial landscape.

data.title: Critical Metals Corp. appoints Project Management Team to accelerate Tanbreez Project development. Nasdaq: CRML.
data.description: On January 30, 2026, Critical Metals Corp. (Nasdaq: CRML) revealed the appointment of a skilled Project Management Team to expedite the advancement of its primary Tanbreez Project. The company, recognized as a prominent critical minerals mining entity, has high hopes for the team's impact on project acceleration and growth.

data.title: Autoliv Inc. recovers almost all tariff costs in Q4, with sales of $2.817 billion surpassing estimates. CEO cites mainly strong growth in India and with Chinese OEMs. The firm cuts headcount by 900, posts an adjusted EPS of $3.19.
data.description: Autoliv, Inc. (NYSE: ALV) reported strong fourth-quarter results with adjusted earnings per share of $3.19, surpassing the analyst estimate of $2.90. The company also revealed quarterly sales of $2.817 billion, exceeding Street expectations of $2.772 billion. Organic net sales increased by 4.2%, mainly due to new product launches. Autoliv's CEO, Mikael Bratt, mentioned a record high sales quarter and full year, driven by significant growth in India and with Chinese OEMs. Sales to COEMs grew by almost 40% in the quarter and by 23% for the full year. The company recovered nearly all tariff costs in Q4 and over 80% for the full year. Despite a 3.9% rise in gross profit to $572 million, operating income decreased by 9.6% to $319 million. The operating margin contracted due to margin pressure. Autoliv also reduced its headcount by about 900 year over year.

data.title: Francisco Partners acquires Jamf for $2.2 billion, at $13.05 per share in cash. CEO excited about milestone.
data.description: In Minneapolis, leading Apple at work management company, Jamf, has been officially acquired by Francisco Partners for $13.05 per share in cash. The acquisition totals around $2.2 billion in enterprise value. CEO of Jamf, John Strosahl, expressed enthusiasm for this development, highlighting it as a significant milestone for the company. Jamf's platform is well-regarded for assisting organizations in effectively managing and securing their Apple devices across various sizes of enterprises.

data.title: Stock futures drop as Trump nominates Kevin Warsh for new Fed Chair. PPI rose 0.5% in Dec, 3% in 2025. Indexes down: Dow -0.84%, S&P -0.92%, Nasdaq -1.13%. SanDisk (+20.53%) up.
data.description: US stock futures dropped on Friday after a mixed close on Thursday, with major benchmark indices lower. President Trump nominated Kevin Warsh to succeed Jerome Powell as Chair of the Federal Reserve. The Producer Price Index rose by 0.5% in December after a delay caused by a government shutdown, ending the year with a 3.0% increase for 2025. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.26% and the two-year bond was at 3.56%. The FedWatch tool projected an 84.7% likelihood of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in March. Index Performance: Dow Jones -0.84%, S&P 500 -0.92%, Nasdaq 100 -1.13%, Russell 2000 -1.51%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust was down 0.32% at $691.84, and the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF declined 0.46% to $626.52. Apple stock was 0.34% lower despite strong financial results for Q1 of fiscal 2026. SanDisk Corp surged 20.53% after posting positive results.

data.title: StorageVault to repay 5.75% hybrid debentures due Jan 31, 2026.
data.description: StorageVault Corporation has confirmed the repayment at maturity of its 5.75% Senior Unsecured Hybrid Debentures, which were due on January 31, 2026. This repayment marks the conclusion of the debentures' term and stands as a significant financial milestone for the company. These debentures represented a key aspect of StorageVault's financial structure, totaling a substantial amount. The successful repayment of these debentures reflects the company's stable financial position and its commitment to fulfilling its financial obligations in a timely manner.

data.title: GDS Holdings has secured a US$300 million private placement with a Chinese investor, for data center expansion.
data.description: In Shanghai, China, on January 30, 2026, GDS Holdings Limited (NASDAQ: GDS; HKEX: 9698), a prominent developer and operator of high-performance data centers in China, revealed a private placement deal totaling US$300 million of Series B convertible preferred shares to Huatai Capital Investment Limited, a Chinese institutional investor. The investment, known as the Private Placement, is intended to aid in the expansion of GDS's data center capacity and for general corporate objectives.
